name: Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA)

colors.symbolism: the blood of Ukrainians spilled in the struggle for independence
the black earth of Ukraine on which that blood was spilled

description: A horizontal bicolour of red over black — the battle flag of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army.
history: The Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA — Ukrayinska Povstanska Armiya) was the partisan army of the Bandera faction of the Organisation of Ukrainian Nationalists (OUN-B), formed in Volyn in 1942–43 and officially dated to 14 October 1942. The UPA fought a guerrilla war against Nazi Germany, the Soviet Union, communist Poland and Czechoslovakia through World War II and into the early 1950s. The red-and-black flag was the UPA's battle standard and survives today as a symbol of Ukrainian nationalism, widely flown in Ukraine since 1991 and especially since the 2022 Russian invasion. The flag is sometimes glossed in the Ukrainian phrase 'червона калина' (red guelder rose) — Ukrainian red blood on Ukrainian black earth.

features.symbolism: Ukrainian red blood spilled on Ukrainian black earth
